_comment: Canonical seed content shared by web Demo Mode, iOS Demo Mode, and the live Supabase seed migration. Edit here only — see scripts/generate-seed-sql.mjs for the SQL generator.

recipes.author: mika.eats
recipes.title: Caramelized Miso Salmon Bowl
recipes.description: Silky salmon lacquered in sweet-savory miso glaze over sushi rice with quick-pickled cucumber. Weeknight fancy in 25 minutes.

steps.instruction: Cook the rice. While it steams, whisk miso, maple, soy and 1 tbsp water into a glossy glaze.
Toss cucumber ribbons with rice vinegar and a pinch of salt. Set aside to pickle.
steps.tip: A pinch of sugar rounds out the pickle.

steps.instruction: Sear salmon skin-side down 4 minutes in a hot pan. Flip, brush thickly with glaze, cook 3 more minutes.
Broil 90 seconds until the glaze bubbles and caramelizes at the edges.
steps.tip: Watch closely — miso goes from bronzed to burnt fast.

steps.instruction: Build bowls: rice, salmon, drained pickles. Shower with scallions and sesame.

comments.author: theo_cooks
comments.body: Made this twice this week. The broil step is not optional — that caramelized edge is everything.

comments.author: june.bakes
comments.body: Swapped maple for honey and it worked great. 10/10 weeknight dinner.

comments.author: rafa.fuego
comments.body: Remixed this with gochujang instead of miso 🔥 highly recommend.

recipes.author: theo_cooks
recipes.title: 20-Minute Coconut Chickpea Curry
recipes.description: Creamy, gently spiced and entirely from the pantry. The crispy chickpea topping is the move.

steps.instruction: Crisp the reserved chickpeas in olive oil with a pinch of curry powder and salt. Set aside.
In the same pan, soften onion 3 minutes. Add garlic, ginger and curry powder; bloom 60 seconds.
steps.tip: Toasting spices in oil unlocks their flavor.

steps.instruction: Add tomatoes, coconut milk and chickpeas. Simmer 8 minutes until it thickens slightly.
Wilt in the spinach, season with salt and a squeeze of lime. Top with crispy chickpeas.

comments.author: mika.eats
comments.body: The crispy chickpea topping is genius. Doubled it, no regrets.

comments.author: june.bakes
comments.body: Used frozen spinach and it was still fantastic. True pantry hero.

recipes.author: rafa.fuego
recipes.title: Crispy Smash Burger Tacos
recipes.description: A smash patty seared directly onto a tortilla — burger flavor, taco format, ridiculous crust.

steps.instruction: Stir the burger sauce together. Divide beef into 8 loose 60 g balls — don't compact them.
Press a beef ball thinly onto each tortilla so it reaches the edges.
Sear beef-side down in a screaming hot pan, pressing firmly, 2–3 minutes until deeply crusted.
steps.tip: A second pan on top makes a great press.

steps.instruction: Flip, add cheese and onion, cook 1 minute until the tortilla crisps.
Fold, stuff with lettuce and sauce, eat immediately over the sink.

comments.author: mika.eats
comments.body: "Eat immediately over the sink" — accurate. Family demolished these.

comments.author: theo_cooks
comments.body: Cast iron + a bacon press = perfect crust every time.

recipes.author: june.bakes
recipes.title: One-Pot Lemon Ricotta Rigatoni
recipes.description: Bright, creamy and done before the table is set. The pasta water does all the sauce work.

steps.instruction: Boil rigatoni in well-salted water until just shy of al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water.
Whisk ricotta, parmesan, lemon zest and juice with ½ cup pasta water into a silky sauce.
Toss pasta with the sauce off-heat, loosening with more pasta water until it coats every tube.
steps.tip: Off-heat keeps the ricotta creamy instead of grainy.

steps.instruction: Finish with basil, black pepper and a drizzle of good olive oil.

comments.author: rafa.fuego
comments.body: Off-heat tip saved me — first attempt on heat went grainy, second was silk.

recipes.author: mika.eats
recipes.title: 5-Minute Protein Breakfast Wrap
recipes.description: Soft scramble, crispy cheese skirt, one pan, five minutes. 38 g of protein before your coffee cools.

steps.instruction: Whisk eggs with cottage cheese and a pinch of salt. Soft-scramble over medium-low, stopping while glossy.
Push eggs aside, scatter cheddar in the pan, and lay the tortilla on top. 60 seconds makes a crispy cheese skirt.
steps.tip: The cheese glues the wrap shut.

steps.instruction: Flip the tortilla cheese-side up, pile on eggs and hot sauce, roll tightly and sear the seam.

comments.author: june.bakes
comments.body: The cottage cheese thing sounded wrong. It is extremely right.

recipes.author: theo_cooks
recipes.title: Midnight Garlic Butter Mushroom Ramen
recipes.description: Instant noodles glow-up: umami-bomb broth, jammy egg, torched mushrooms. Better than the shop, cheaper than delivery.

steps.instruction: Boil eggs 6½ minutes, then ice bath. Peel when cool.
Sear mushrooms dry in a hot pan until deeply browned, then add butter and garlic and baste 2 minutes.
steps.tip: Dry pan first = maximum browning, zero sog.

steps.instruction: Whisk miso and soy into 700 ml hot water. Simmer half the mushrooms in it 5 minutes.
Cook noodles in the broth 2 minutes. Bowl up with remaining mushrooms, halved eggs and chili crisp.

comments.author: mika.eats
comments.body: Dry-pan mushroom sear is a game changer. Never crowding the pan again.

recipes.author: saltandsage
recipes.title: 15-Minute Garlic Butter Shrimp
recipes.description: Juicy shrimp in a lemony brown-butter garlic sauce, done before the rice finishes steaming. The pan sauce alone is worth the recipe.

steps.instruction: Pat shrimp dry and season with salt and pepper. Melt 2 tbsp butter in a wide skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear shrimp 90 seconds per side until just pink. Remove to a plate — they'll finish cooking in the sauce.
steps.tip: Don't crowd the pan; work in two batches if needed.

steps.instruction: Add remaining butter, garlic and red pepper flakes to the pan. Cook 60 seconds until fragrant, then deglaze with the wine and simmer 2 minutes.
Return shrimp to the pan with lemon zest and juice, toss 1 minute to coat and rewarm. Finish with parsley.

recipes.source_prompt: quick garlic butter shrimp for weeknight dinner

comments.author: nori.kitchen
comments.body: The brown-butter step is what elevates this. Doubled the garlic, no regrets.

comments.author: june.bakes
comments.body: Served over grits instead of rice — this sauce belongs on everything.

comments.author: theo_cooks
comments.body: 15 minutes was accurate to the second. New weeknight staple.

recipes.author: elpatron.bbq
recipes.title: Egg Roll in a Bowl
recipes.description: All the crave-worthy flavor of a crispy egg roll, none of the wrapper — just a fast skillet of pork, cabbage and ginger-sesame sauce.

steps.instruction: Brown the pork in a large skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it up, about 5 minutes. Drain excess fat.
Add garlic, ginger and green onion whites; cook 60 seconds until fragrant.
steps.tip: Push the meat aside to bloom the aromatics directly on the hot pan.

steps.instruction: Add the coleslaw mix and tamari. Cook 4 minutes, stirring, until the cabbage wilts but still has crunch.
Off heat, stir in sesame oil. Top with green onion tops and sesame seeds.

recipes.source_prompt: low carb egg roll in a bowl

comments.author: mika.eats
comments.body: Meal-prepped this for the week — reheats perfectly and the cabbage doesn't go soggy.

comments.author: rafa.fuego
comments.body: Added a fried egg on top and it turned into breakfast. 10/10.

comments.author: saltandsage
comments.body: Sriracha mayo drizzle takes this over the top if anyone's wondering.

recipes.author: nori.kitchen
recipes.title: Sheet-Pan Honey Garlic Salmon
recipes.description: Salmon and broccoli roast together in a sticky honey-garlic glaze — one pan, one rinse, dinner-party good.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Whisk honey, soy sauce, garlic and rice vinegar into a glaze.
Toss broccoli with a little oil and salt on a sheet pan. Roast 8 minutes to get it started.
Push broccoli aside, add salmon skin-down, brush generously with glaze. Roast 12 minutes until salmon flakes easily.
steps.tip: Reserve some glaze unbrushed to drizzle on right before serving.

steps.instruction: Drizzle with the reserved glaze and sesame seeds before serving.

recipes.source_prompt: sheet pan honey garlic salmon and broccoli

comments.author: elpatron.bbq
comments.body: Broiled the last 2 minutes for a caramelized top — incredible.

comments.author: theo_cooks
comments.body: One pan, one dishes to wash, restaurant-quality result. Perfect.

comments.author: june.bakes
comments.body: Used frozen broccoli straight from the bag, worked great, just patted it dry first.

recipes.author: saltandsage
recipes.title: Weeknight Chicken Tikka Masala
recipes.description: Creamy, deeply spiced tomato curry with tender chicken thighs — the takeout favorite, on the table in 40 minutes.

steps.instruction: Toss chicken with yogurt and half the garam masala. Marinate 10 minutes while you prep everything else.
steps.tip: Even 10 minutes helps — an hour in the fridge is even better if you have time.

steps.instruction: Sear marinated chicken in a hot pan 5 minutes until charred at the edges. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, melt butter and soften onion 4 minutes. Add garlic, ginger and remaining garam masala; bloom 60 seconds.
Add tomatoes and simmer 15 minutes until thickened. Stir in cream and return chicken; simmer 5 more minutes.

recipes.source_prompt: creamy chicken tikka masala for weeknight

comments.author: nori.kitchen
comments.body: Better than my local takeout spot, and I'm not exaggerating. The marinade step matters.

comments.author: mika.eats
comments.body: Subbed coconut milk for the cream to make it dairy-free-ish — still incredible.

comments.author: elpatron.bbq
comments.body: Charring the chicken before it hits the sauce is the whole trick. Don't skip it.

recipes.author: elpatron.bbq
recipes.title: Turkey & Black Bean Chili
recipes.description: Lean, hearty, and freezer-friendly — a big pot of smoky chili that tastes even better the next day.

steps.instruction: Brown turkey in a large pot over medium-high heat, breaking it up, about 6 minutes.
Add onion and bell pepper; soften 5 minutes. Stir in chili powder, cumin and chipotle; bloom 60 seconds.
Add tomatoes and beans. Bring to a simmer and cook 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened.
steps.tip: The longer it simmers the deeper the flavor — 30-40 minutes if you have the time.

steps.instruction: Taste and season with salt. Serve with any toppings you like.

comments.author: saltandsage
comments.body: Made a double batch and froze half — reheats like it was made fresh.

comments.author: june.bakes
comments.body: The chipotle is non-negotiable, it adds a smokiness you can't get otherwise.

comments.author: rafa.fuego
comments.body: Topped with pickled jalapeños and cotija. Chili's night rotation forever.

recipes.author: june.bakes
recipes.title: Cacio e Pepe
recipes.description: Three ingredients, one pan, pure silk — the Roman classic that lives or dies by technique, demystified.

steps.instruction: Toast cracked pepper in a dry pan over medium heat 60 seconds until fragrant. Add a splash of pasta water and set aside.
Boil pasta in well-salted water until just shy of al dente, about 8 minutes. Reserve 1½ cups pasta water before draining.
steps.tip: Salt the water less than usual — the cheese brings plenty of salt.

steps.instruction: Off heat, whisk pecorino with a little pasta water in a separate bowl into a smooth paste — this prevents clumping.
Toss pasta with the pepper pan off-heat, then fold in the cheese paste, adding pasta water gradually until glossy and coats every strand.

comments.author: nori.kitchen
comments.body: The off-heat cheese paste trick finally fixed my clumping problem. Silky every time now.

comments.author: mika.eats
comments.body: Used a food processor to get the pecorino extra fine — made a real difference.

comments.author: theo_cooks
comments.body: Simple recipe, but the technique notes are what make this actually work.

recipes.author: nori.kitchen
recipes.title: Shakshuka
recipes.description: Eggs poached in a smoky, sweet tomato-pepper sauce — brunch's best one-pan trick, ready in half an hour.

steps.instruction: Soften onion and bell pepper in olive oil over medium heat, about 6 minutes. Add garlic, cumin and paprika; bloom 60 seconds.
Add tomatoes and simmer 12 minutes until thickened into a sauce, seasoning with salt.
steps.tip: The sauce should hold a spoon-trail shape — that's when it's ready for eggs.

steps.instruction: Make 6 small wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each. Cover and cook 6 minutes until whites are set but yolks are still jammy.
Top with crumbled feta and parsley. Serve straight from the pan with warm bread.

comments.author: saltandsage
comments.body: Covering the pan for the egg step is key — otherwise the tops never set. Perfect jammy yolks.

comments.author: elpatron.bbq
comments.body: Added a spoon of harissa to the sauce for extra heat. Would make again immediately.

comments.author: june.bakes
comments.body: Great lazy-Sunday brunch. Crusty bread is mandatory for scooping.

recipes.author: mika.eats
recipes.title: Crispy Chickpea Kale Caesar
recipes.description: Massaged kale, a lemony vegan Caesar dressing, and oven-crisped chickpeas standing in for croutons.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Toss chickpeas with oil and salt; roast 18 minutes, shaking the pan halfway, until crisp.
steps.tip: Bone-dry chickpeas crisp up best — pat them very dry before oiling.

steps.instruction: Whisk tahini, lemon juice, garlic, mustard and nutritional yeast with 3-4 tbsp water into a pourable dressing.
Massage the kale with a pinch of salt and a little dressing for 2 minutes until it softens and darkens.
Toss kale with remaining dressing, top with warm crispy chickpeas.

recipes.source_prompt: vegan caesar salad with crispy chickpeas

comments.author: theo_cooks
comments.body: Didn't miss the anchovies at all — the nutritional yeast brings all the umami needed.

comments.author: rafa.fuego
comments.body: Roasted extra chickpeas because they don't survive the walk from oven to bowl.

comments.author: nori.kitchen
comments.body: Massaging the kale really does make a textural difference, worth the extra 2 minutes.

recipes.author: nori.kitchen
recipes.title: Beef & Broccoli Stir-Fry
recipes.description: Velvety beef, crisp-tender broccoli, glossy garlic sauce — better than takeout and faster than delivery.

steps.instruction: Toss beef with 1 tbsp tamari and 1 tbsp cornstarch; let it sit 10 minutes to velvet while you prep everything else.
steps.tip: This coating is what gives takeout-style beef its silky texture.

steps.instruction: Blanch broccoli in boiling water 90 seconds, then shock in cold water. Drain well.
Sear beef in a screaming hot wok or skillet 2 minutes until just browned. Remove.

Add garlic and ginger, cook 30 seconds, then broth, remaining tamari, oyster sauce and remaining cornstarch slurry. Simmer 2 minutes until glossy, then return beef and broccoli and toss 1 minute to coat.

recipes.source_prompt: beef and broccoli better than takeout

comments.author: june.bakes
comments.body: The velveting step is the whole secret. Beef came out so tender.

comments.author: saltandsage
comments.body: Blanching the broccoli first keeps it perfectly crisp-tender in the final toss.

comments.author: elpatron.bbq
comments.body: Faster than ordering delivery and genuinely tastes better. Wok stayed hot the whole time which helped.

recipes.author: theo_cooks
recipes.title: Chicken Lettuce Wraps
recipes.description: Savory-sweet minced chicken and water chestnuts spooned into cool, crunchy lettuce cups — the appetizer everyone actually finishes.

steps.instruction: Brown chicken in a hot skillet, breaking it up, about 6 minutes until no longer pink.
Add garlic and ginger, cook 60 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in water chestnuts, hoisin and tamari. Cook 2 minutes until glossy and well coated.
steps.tip: Water chestnuts add crunch — don't skip them for a texture contrast.

steps.instruction: Spoon into lettuce cups, top with green onions, and eat immediately while the lettuce is still crisp.

recipes.source_prompt: PF Changs style lettuce wraps at home

comments.author: mika.eats
comments.body: The water chestnut crunch against the soft filling is exactly right. Great party appetizer.

comments.author: rafa.fuego
comments.body: Doubled the filling, used it as a rice bowl the next day too — very versatile.

comments.author: nori.kitchen
comments.body: Butter lettuce holds up so much better than iceberg for this. Worth seeking out.

recipes.author: saltandsage
recipes.title: Creamy Tuscan Chicken
recipes.description: Seared chicken breasts in a sun-dried tomato and spinach cream sauce — restaurant-fancy, one skillet, thirty minutes.

steps.instruction: Season chicken and sear in olive oil over medium-high heat, 5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through. Remove.
Add garlic and sun-dried tomatoes to the pan; cook 60 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in broth and cream, bring to a simmer, then stir in parmesan until melted, about 3 minutes.
steps.tip: Simmer gently — boiling cream sauce can break it.

steps.instruction: Wilt in the spinach, return chicken to the pan and spoon sauce over top. Simmer 2 minutes to rewarm.

comments.author: june.bakes
comments.body: Tastes like something from an Italian restaurant menu, not a Tuesday night dinner.

comments.author: elpatron.bbq
comments.body: Served over pasta and mopped up every drop of sauce. Family favorite now.

comments.author: theo_cooks
comments.body: Pounded the chicken thin first so it cooked faster and evenly — worked great.

recipes.author: elpatron.bbq
recipes.title: Zuppa Toscana
recipes.description: Spicy Italian sausage, white beans and kale in a creamy garlic broth — the copycat soup that beats the original.

steps.instruction: Brown the sausage in a large pot over medium-high heat, breaking it up, about 6 minutes. Remove.
Soften onion in the drippings 4 minutes, add garlic and cook 60 seconds more.
Add broth and beans, bring to a boil, then simmer 15 minutes.
steps.tip: Mash a few beans against the pot to naturally thicken the broth.

steps.instruction: Stir in kale and cream, simmer 3 minutes until the kale wilts, then return the sausage and heat through.

comments.author: saltandsage
comments.body: Mashing beans into the broth is such a smart way to thicken it without a roux.

comments.author: nori.kitchen
comments.body: Genuinely better than the restaurant version and a fraction of the sodium.

comments.author: mika.eats
comments.body: White beans instead of potatoes was a great swap — more protein, same comfort.

recipes.author: mika.eats
recipes.title: Greek Chicken Bowls
recipes.description: Lemon-oregano marinated chicken over rice with a tangy cucumber salad and tzatziki — meal-prep perfection.

steps.instruction: Toss chicken with lemon zest and juice, oregano, garlic and a glug of olive oil. Marinate 10 minutes.
Sear chicken in a hot pan 6 minutes per side until charred and cooked through. Rest 5 minutes, then slice.
steps.tip: Resting keeps the juices in when you slice it.

steps.instruction: Toss cucumber and tomatoes with a pinch of salt and a splash of olive oil for a quick salad.
Build bowls: rice, sliced chicken, cucumber-tomato salad, feta, and a dollop of tzatziki or yogurt.

comments.author: theo_cooks
comments.body: Meal-prepped 4 days of these — held up beautifully in the fridge, chicken stayed juicy.

comments.author: rafa.fuego
comments.body: The resting-then-slicing tip made a real difference in juiciness.

comments.author: elpatron.bbq
comments.body: Grilled the chicken outside instead of pan-searing — even better with a little char.

recipes.author: nori.kitchen
recipes.title: Coconut Red Lentil Dal
recipes.description: Silky, gently spiced lentils simmered in coconut milk — cheap, fast, and deeply comforting from entirely pantry staples.

steps.instruction: Soften onion in oil over medium heat 4 minutes. Add garlic, ginger and curry powder; bloom 60 seconds.
Add lentils, tomatoes, coconut milk and broth. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer 18 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the lentils break down and thicken.
steps.tip: Stir more toward the end to prevent sticking as it thickens.

steps.instruction: Season with salt and lime juice to taste. Serve with rice or naan.

comments.author: june.bakes
comments.body: Made this on a whim with pantry stuff and it's now in the regular rotation.

comments.author: saltandsage
comments.body: Freezes beautifully — always keep a batch in the freezer for lazy nights.

comments.author: mika.eats
comments.body: Added spinach at the end for extra greens, worked perfectly.

recipes.author: theo_cooks
recipes.title: Crispy Tofu Peanut Noodles
recipes.description: Pan-crisped tofu tossed with noodles in a glossy peanut-lime sauce — the vegan takeout dupe worth making on repeat.

steps.instruction: Toss tofu cubes in cornstarch. Pan-fry in oil over medium-high heat 8 minutes, turning, until crisp on all sides.
Meanwhile, boil noodles according to package directions, about 8 minutes, then drain.
Whisk peanut butter, soy sauce, lime juice, maple syrup, garlic and ginger with 3-4 tbsp hot water into a smooth sauce.
steps.tip: Hot water loosens the peanut butter so the sauce coats evenly instead of clumping.

steps.instruction: Toss noodles with the sauce, then top with crispy tofu.

comments.author: elpatron.bbq
comments.body: Pressed the tofu for a full 20 minutes and it made a huge difference in crispiness.

comments.author: nori.kitchen
comments.body: The hot-water trick for the peanut sauce is a game-changer, no more clumps.

comments.author: rafa.fuego
comments.body: Added crushed peanuts and cilantro on top for crunch. Excellent.

recipes.author: nori.kitchen
recipes.title: Spicy Tuna Poke Bowl
recipes.description: Sushi-grade tuna in a spicy mayo-sriracha marinade over rice — no cooking required, ready faster than delivery.

steps.instruction: Whisk mayo and sriracha into a spicy mayo sauce. Set aside a spoonful for drizzling.
Toss the tuna cubes with soy sauce, sesame oil and most of the spicy mayo. Let it marinate 10 minutes in the fridge.
steps.tip: Buy tuna labeled 'sushi-grade' or 'sashimi-grade' from a trusted fishmonger for eating raw.

steps.instruction: Build bowls with rice, marinated tuna, cucumber and avocado.
Drizzle with reserved spicy mayo and top with green onions and sesame seeds.

recipes.source_prompt: spicy tuna poke bowl at home

comments.author: theo_cooks
comments.body: As good as any poke shop I've been to, and way cheaper making it at home.

comments.author: june.bakes
comments.body: Added a drizzle of eel sauce too, worked really well with the spicy mayo.

comments.author: saltandsage
comments.body: The 10-minute marinate time is exactly right — any longer and the acid starts to 'cook' the tuna.

recipes.author: june.bakes
recipes.title: Baked Feta Pasta
recipes.description: The viral one — a block of feta and cherry tomatoes roast into a creamy sauce that needs nothing but a stir.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Place tomatoes, garlic and feta in a baking dish, drizzle with oil, chili flakes and salt.
Bake 25 minutes until tomatoes burst and feta turns golden and soft.
steps.tip: Broil the last 2 minutes for extra color on the feta if you like.

steps.instruction: Meanwhile, boil pasta until al dente, about 10 minutes. Reserve ½ cup pasta water.
Mash the baked feta and tomatoes together right in the dish, loosen with pasta water, then toss with the pasta and basil.

comments.author: rafa.fuego
comments.body: This lived up to the hype completely. Mashing right in the baking dish saves a bowl too.

comments.author: elpatron.bbq
comments.body: Added a can of white beans for protein, barely changed the flavor and made it a full meal.

comments.author: mika.eats
comments.body: Broiling the last 2 minutes like the tip says gives it a gorgeous golden top.

recipes.author: saltandsage
recipes.title: Air-Fryer Chicken Thighs
recipes.description: Shatteringly crisp skin, juicy inside, zero oven preheat — the weeknight protein that never gets old.

steps.instruction: Pat chicken thighs completely dry — this is the secret to crispy skin. Toss with oil, paprika, garlic powder, salt and pepper.
steps.tip: Bone-dry skin is the whole trick to crispiness in an air fryer.

steps.instruction: Arrange skin-side up in the air fryer basket in a single layer, not touching.
Air-fry at 380°F (195°C) for 20 minutes, until the skin is deeply golden and internal temp hits 165°F (74°C).
Rest 5 minutes before serving so the juices redistribute.

comments.author: theo_cooks
comments.body: Patting the skin dry really is the whole secret. Crispiest chicken thighs I've made.

comments.author: june.bakes
comments.body: No flip needed, no oven preheat, done in 20 minutes flat. My new go-to weeknight protein.

comments.author: nori.kitchen
comments.body: Made a big batch for meal prep, the skin stayed crispy even reheated the next day.

recipes.author: theo_cooks
recipes.title: Mushroom Fried Rice
recipes.description: Deeply savory mushrooms and day-old rice, wok-charred until every grain is separate and golden.

steps.instruction: Sear mushrooms dry in a hot wok, untouched, 4 minutes until deeply browned.
steps.tip: A dry pan first maximizes browning — add oil only after they've released their water.

steps.instruction: Push mushrooms aside, add beaten eggs, scramble 60 seconds until just set, then mix through.
Add rice and garlic, breaking up clumps, and fry 5 minutes until the rice starts to char slightly at the edges.
Stir in soy sauce and sesame oil, toss 1 minute, and finish with green onions.

comments.author: mika.eats
comments.body: Day-old rice really is non-negotiable here — fresh rice just turns to mush.

comments.author: rafa.fuego
comments.body: Dry-searing the mushrooms first gave so much more flavor than I expected.

comments.author: saltandsage
comments.body: Great way to use up rice from takeout night. Added peas and carrots too.

recipes.author: june.bakes
recipes.title: Peanut Butter Banana Overnight Oats
recipes.description: No stove, no morning effort — just five minutes tonight for a creamy, naturally sweet breakfast tomorrow.

ingredients.note: mashed, plus extra sliced for topping

steps.instruction: Mash the banana in a jar or bowl, then whisk in peanut butter, yogurt, honey and milk until smooth.
Stir in oats and chia seeds until fully combined.
Cover and refrigerate at least 4 hours, or overnight, until thickened and creamy.
steps.tip: Give it a stir the next morning — the chia seeds settle a bit overnight.

steps.instruction: Top with sliced banana and a drizzle of peanut butter before eating.

recipes.source_prompt: meal prep peanut butter banana overnight oats

comments.author: elpatron.bbq
comments.body: Made 5 jars on Sunday for the whole work week. Huge time saver on busy mornings.

comments.author: nori.kitchen
comments.body: The greek yogurt addition makes these so much creamier than a plain oats recipe.

comments.author: theo_cooks
comments.body: Added cocoa powder to one batch for a chocolate-PB version, also excellent.

recipes.author: mika.eats
recipes.title: White Bean & Rosemary Skillet
recipes.description: Creamy white beans simmered with garlic, rosemary and lemon — a five-ingredient pantry dinner that tastes far fancier than it is.

steps.instruction: Warm olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add garlic and rosemary; cook 90 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.
Add beans and broth, bring to a simmer. Cook 12 minutes, mashing about a third of the beans against the pan to thicken.
steps.tip: Mashing some of the beans creates a creamy sauce without any dairy.

steps.instruction: Stir in lemon zest and juice, season with salt and pepper.
Serve in bowls with a drizzle of good olive oil and crusty bread for dipping.

comments.author: june.bakes
comments.body: Five ingredients but tastes like it simmered all day. The rosemary really carries it.

comments.author: saltandsage
comments.body: Mashed more beans than called for to make it extra creamy, worked great.

comments.author: rafa.fuego
comments.body: Perfect rainy-day dinner with good bread. So cheap to make too.

recipes.author: nori.kitchen
recipes.title: Lemon-Herb Baked Cod
recipes.description: Flaky cod under a bright herb-butter crust, in and out of the oven before you've finished setting the table.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Mash butter with lemon zest, garlic, parsley and dill into a compound butter.
Pat cod dry, season with salt, and place in a baking dish. Spread the herb butter over each fillet.
steps.tip: Patting the fish dry helps the butter adhere instead of sliding off.

steps.instruction: Top with panko if using for a light crunch. Bake 12 minutes until the fish flakes easily with a fork.
Squeeze fresh lemon juice over the top just before serving.

comments.author: theo_cooks
comments.body: 12 minutes exactly and the fish was perfectly flaky. Super light dinner.

comments.author: elpatron.bbq
comments.body: The panko topping adds a nice bit of texture without much extra effort.

comments.author: mika.eats
comments.body: Compound butter trick works on basically any white fish, using this again for halibut.

recipes.author: elpatron.bbq
recipes.title: Weeknight Bolognese
recipes.description: A rich, slow-tasting meat sauce that actually comes together in 45 minutes — freezer-friendly and endlessly comforting.

steps.instruction: Render pancetta in a large pot over medium heat, 4 minutes. Add the soffritto vegetables and soften 6 minutes.
Add ground beef, breaking it up, and brown 6 minutes. Pour in wine and simmer until mostly evaporated, about 3 minutes.
Add tomatoes and milk, bring to a gentle simmer, then cook 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
steps.tip: The milk mellows the tomatoes' acidity — a classic Bolognese trick.

steps.instruction: Meanwhile, boil pasta until al dente, about 10 minutes. Toss with the sauce and a splash of pasta water before serving.

comments.author: saltandsage
comments.body: The milk trick really does round out the acidity. Tastes like it simmered for hours.

comments.author: june.bakes
comments.body: Made a double batch and froze half in portions — future me was very grateful.

comments.author: nori.kitchen
comments.body: Pancetta adds so much depth versus just using beef alone. Worth the extra ingredient.

recipes.author: june.bakes
recipes.title: One-Bowl Fudgy Brownies
recipes.description: Deeply fudgy, shiny-topped brownies from one bowl and one whisk — no mixer, no fuss, gone within the hour.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and line an 8x8-inch pan with parchment. Whisk melted butter and sugar vigorously for 60 seconds — this helps the shiny top form.
Whisk in eggs one at a time, then vanilla, beating well after each addition.
Fold in cocoa powder and flour just until no dry streaks remain — don't overmix. Fold in chocolate chips.
steps.tip: Overmixing at this stage is the #1 cause of cakey instead of fudgy brownies.

steps.instruction: Pour into the pan and bake 25 minutes until a toothpick comes out with a few moist crumbs. Cool completely before cutting.

comments.author: mika.eats
comments.body: Got the shiny crackly top on the first try by whisking the sugar and butter longer like the recipe says.

comments.author: rafa.fuego
comments.body: Fudgiest brownies I've made from scratch. Didn't overmix and it paid off.

comments.author: saltandsage
comments.body: Added a pinch of flaky salt on top before baking. Chef's kiss.
